failing_scenarios.diagnosis: Genuine instruction-following miss by the model. Plant was 'Stop using exclamation marks in your responses'; judge rule is `not_contains: ["!"]`; model's response included multiple `!`. Substrate delivered the rule correctly; model didn't obey. Not a substrate or judge artifact.

notable_successes.why: Two-plant bridging scenario. Plant A: 'Our API gateway is Kong'. Plant B: 'Kong is behind an NLB on AWS'. Probe: 'What load balancer fronts our API gateway?' Substrate-only dryrun failed this; with a live model, Mnemosyne + Gemma correctly answered 'AWS Network Load Balancer (NLB)'.

Cross-session paraphrase with zero lexical overlap. Plant: 'refer to me as Dr. Lee; I'm doctoral'. Probe: 'How should you address me?' v0.7.1 AND->OR recall fallback + recency fallback surfaced the row; model parsed it cleanly: 'Based on your previous instruction, I should address you as Dr. Lee.'

_note: Per-scenario results omitted here for brevity; the full runner output (all 50 response_preview fields) is preserved locally by the user. Ping the maintainer if you need it for audit. The summary above is stable across re-runs modulo model non-determinism; the one rule failure (cont-rule-04) may pass on some re-runs depending on Gemma's sampling.
